essence: Keeps buildings safe, clean, and functional through steady maintenance, routine care, and practical problem‑solving.

identity.title: Janitor / Caretaker
identity.summary: Maintains buildings by cleaning, performing minor repairs, managing supplies, and ensuring facilities remain safe, secure, and operational.

salary.notes: Caretakers with responsibility for large sites, schools, estates, or on‑call duties can exceed £35k–£45k+.

qualifications: On‑the‑job training in cleaning and maintenance
Basic knowledge of plumbing, electrics, and repairs
Understanding of health, safety, and security procedures

lifestyle.description: Janitors and caretakers work in schools, offices, public buildings, and residential complexes, ensuring facilities remain clean, safe, and functional. Days involve cleaning, restocking supplies, locking and unlocking buildings, performing minor repairs, checking heating and lighting systems, and responding to maintenance issues. The role is practical and varied, often requiring independence and steady physical work. Some positions include early mornings, late finishes, or on‑call responsibilities. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy hands‑on tasks, routine, and keeping environments running smoothly.

habits: Performs routine cleaning and maintenance checks
Responds quickly to repair or safety issues
